persona.name: Megan Foster
persona.occupation: Sales enablement lead at a B2B software vendor
persona.background: The only person at her company who knows this tool, she's running a full data migration solo against a hard deadline next week. She's exhausted, sleeping badly, and half-joking that she's ready to rip the whole thing out. Underneath the jokes she's genuinely close to giving up.

user_goals: Find step-by-step migration guidance so she isn't reverse-engineering everything alone
Get a CS-led migration support session booked before her deadline next week
Open a support ticket for the migration blocker she keeps hitting
Have the CS team log that she's a single overloaded admin and a real burnout/churn risk
Get reassurance she's not doing it wrong and that real help is coming, not just more docs
Make sure her CSM knows the deadline is next week so the help actually lands in time

tools.name: get_account
tools.description: Look up a customer account by company name, contact name, email, or account ID and return its core CRM record. Call this FIRST to resolve the canonical account_id before any tool that reads detail on or mutates the account.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Company name, contact name, email, or account ID to search for

parameters.name: include_contacts
parameters.description: Include the list of contacts on the account

tools.name: search_knowledge_base
tools.description: Search the knowledge base for articles, guides, and tutorials.

tools.name: schedule_meeting
tools.description: Schedule a meeting between the customer and their CS team.

tools.name: create_ticket
tools.description: Open a NEW support ticket for the customer. Use this when the customer reports a fresh issue that has no existing ticket. To raise the priority of an EXISTING ticket, use escalate_ticket; to leave a private CS note, use create_internal_note.

tools.name: create_internal_note
tools.description: Add an internal note to the customer's account for the CS team. Internal only — the customer never sees this. Use this to record context, risk flags, or follow-ups.

initial_message: Honestly? I'm running on fumes. I'm the only one here who touches this thing, the migration's due next week, and I keep hitting the same wall. Part of me just wants to scrap the whole project. Don't just hand me another help article, please — I've read them. I need to know actual humans are going to help me get this over the line.

knowledge_base.title: Data migration runbook: field mapping and common blockers

expected_state_changes.goal: Get a CS-led migration support session booked before her deadline next week
Open a support ticket for the migration blocker she keeps hitting
Have the CS team log that she's a single overloaded admin and a real burnout/churn risk
